Off Calendar Feb-04-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Richard Lee of SELMAN BREITMAN (415-979-0400), appearing for defendant / moving party Milwaukee Valve Company, Inc.
Kimberly Chu of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Willie F. Brown

RULING:
DEFENDANT MILWAUKEE VALVE COMPANY, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Milwaukee Valve Company, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden under either prong of Aguilar. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to products or materials attributable to defendant. Plaintiff's deposition testimony and responses to defendant's special interrogatories preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of negating an essential element of plaintiff's claims. Defendant did not properly make an issue whether any original component part removed from a Milwaukee valve contained asbestos. Plaintiff's objections to Mr. Ocacio's deposition testimony are sustained. Order signed in open court. The Court did not rule on defendant's evidentiary objections, burden not shifted. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Kyle McLean of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #13787 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)

Lindsay Weiss of MANION GAYNOR & MANNING (213-622-7300), appearing for defendant / moving party Temporary Plant Cleaners, Inc.
Kimberly Chu of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Willie F. Brown

RULING:
DEFENDANT TEMPORARY PLANT CLEANERS, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Temporary Plant Cleaners, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is granted. On the issue of causation, defendant sustained its initial burden. Plaintiff failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Specifically, plaintiff failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether the products, materials and/or debris to which defendant's employees exposed him contained asbestos. The court will point out that it did not grant the motion on the issues of workers compensation exclusivity and duty. Defendant's objections to plaintiff's declaration are overruled. Defendant's objections to the statements and opinions in Dr. Compton's declaration regarding asbestos content of the gaskets and packing at issue are sustained. Order signed in open court. Plaintiff's deposition testimony and declaration and Mr. Ay's declaration create a triable issue whether plaintiff was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials (insulation) attributable to defendant. Defendant's objections to plaintiff's declaration are overruled. Defendant's objections to Mr. Ay's declaration are overruled. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Tara A.
